stmt: ∀ {α : Type u} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)] {x : α}, x ≡ ⊠(⊤ : α) ↔ x ≡ ⊠x
∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (⊤ : α) ≡ □(⊤ : α)
There exists a type α with instances `[ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)]` such that `¬ (□(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≡ ⊠(⊤ : α))`.

There exists a three-element linearly ordered type α with elements `0 < a < 1`, equipped with `⊤ = 1`, `⊥ = 0`, `⊠0 = 1`, `⊠a = a`, `⊠1 = a`, `□0 = 0`, `□a = 1`, and `□1 = 1`, such that `[ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α]` holds, `a` is a Gödel fixpoint, and `□(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≠ ⊠(⊤ : α)`.
∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)], □(⊠(⊤ : α)) ≡ ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)], (∀ ⦃x y : α⦄, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → ∀ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 = ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ ⦃x y : α⦄, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 = ⊠(⊤ : α)
∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α] [Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)] {x y : α}, x ≡ y → ⊠x ≡ ⊠y

For every type α equipped with ACR, Prov, Reft, APS, and C5, if `ACR.GödelFixpoint α` is nonempty, then every positive iterate of `⊠` on `(⊤ : α)` is equivalent to `⊠(⊤ : α)`: for every natural number `n` with `1 ≤ n`, the `n`-fold iterate of `⊠` applied to `(⊤ : α)` is equivalent to `⊠(⊤ : α)`.
